decisions.question: Target language & typing for the rebuild?
decisions.rationale: Current lib/ is untyped CommonJS JS. A widely-consumed library benefits most from shipped .d.ts and a typed config schema (schema.json -> config interfaces). User confirmed the TS recommendation. Raise engines.node to a maintained LTS (>=18) and resolve the existing >=14 vs CHANGELOG >=16 inconsistency.

decisions.question: Packaging / module format for the rebuilt library?
decisions.recommended: Dual ESM + CJS with exports map (tsup)
decisions.rationale: rascal has a large installed base of require('rascal') consumers; dual output preserves them while enabling import. Build via tsup/esbuild emitting both formats + .d.ts. User confirmed.

decisions.question: Keep the dual callback Broker + promise BrokerAsPromised API?
decisions.recommended: Keep both surfaces; reimplement the core with async/await
decisions.rationale: The dual public surface (9 exports incl. Broker + BrokerAsPromised) is a [MUST] contract. Preserving both is non-breaking; internals move from async.compose callback chains to native async/await. User confirmed.

decisions.question: Keep amqplib as the AMQP transport?
decisions.rationale: amqplib IS the library's reason to exist; rascal is its config-driven wrapper. Swapping it (e.g. rabbitmq-client) would change channel/connection semantics the whole codebase depends on. Keep as peerDependency. Recorded at recommended (accept-all).

decisions.question: Which helper dependencies to replace vs keep?
decisions.recommended: Replace where native equivalents exist; keep specialized infra libs
decisions.rationale: Replace: async (-> native async/await), uuid@8 (-> crypto.randomUUID), safe-json-parse (-> try/catch JSON.parse), forward-emitter (-> explicit event re-emit), xregexp (-> native named-group regex), and trim lodash to targeted helpers or structuredClone for cloneDeep. Keep: amqplib, generic-pool (channel pooling), lru-cache (counters), debug, stashback (or a small native correlation map). Recorded at recommended (accept-all).

decisions.question: Test framework for the rebuild?
decisions.recommended: Vitest (+ v8 coverage), retain the docker RabbitMQ harness
decisions.rationale: zUnit is niche; Vitest gives TS-native tests, parallelism, and built-in coverage (replacing nyc). Port the 15 existing suites preserving their assertions/test-vectors; keep the real-RabbitMQ docker dependency (the suites are integration-style). Recorded at recommended (accept-all).

decisions.question: REST / GraphQL / tRPC?
decisions.rationale: rascal exposes no HTTP API (brief: 0 endpoints). Its 'API' is the library export surface (RD-3) plus the internal RabbitMQ Management HTTP client. No web contract style applies.

decisions.question: DB engine + ORM?
decisions.rationale: No persistence layer (brief: 0 data models, 0 SQL DDL, no ORM). State is ephemeral in-memory (channel pools, counters, sessions).

decisions.question: Frontend framework?
decisions.rationale: Backend messaging library; no UI layer detected.

decisions.question: Deployment target?
decisions.recommended: npm registry publish (existing GitHub Actions publish workflow)
decisions.rationale: A library is distributed, not hosted. Keep the release-triggered npm publish workflow; update the Node matrix. Recorded at recommended (accept-all).

decisions.question: Preserve the layer boundaries and overall shape?
decisions.recommended: Single package, preserve layer boundaries (config/domain -> service -> messaging -> public API)
decisions.rationale: The discovered layering (lib/config, lib/backoff+counters, lib/amqp + tasks, index.js surface) is clean and maps directly to TS modules. Keep one published package; the task pipeline stays the modular topology-assertion engine. Recorded at recommended (accept-all).

decisions.question: Re-run existing tests or rewrite?
decisions.recommended: Port suites to Vitest preserving assertions; run old + new against the same broker
decisions.rationale: 100% test-layer coverage (15 suites) is the strongest equivalence asset. Port them faithfully; during transition, run the legacy zUnit suite and the new Vitest suite against one RabbitMQ to prove parity. Recorded at recommended (accept-all).

decisions.question: Build order?
decisions.rationale: Import graph foundations: lib/config/* + lib/utils/setTimeoutUnref + lib/counters/* + lib/management/Client (most depended-upon leaves) -> lib/backoff -> lib/amqp/tasks -> Vhost -> Broker -> Publication/Subscription -> BrokerAsPromised wrappers -> index.js surface. Recorded at recommended (accept-all).

decisions.question: How to prove equivalence?
decisions.recommended: Ported integration suite + parallel-run against real RabbitMQ; gate publish on green
decisions.rationale: Equivalence is provable because behavior is asserted against a real broker. Gate: every ported suite green on the Node LTS matrix, dual-format smoke test (require + import), and a parallel old-vs-new run on representative configs before the major release. Recorded at recommended (accept-all).
